message.content:
Got it — here are direct, high-quality YouTube + article resources specifically for setting up OpenClaw with memory, identity, and purpose (the part you’re missing).

🎥 Best YouTube Walkthroughs (watch these)
1. Full OpenClaw Setup (closest to what you need)

Search: “OpenClaw full setup memory identity tutorial”

Recommended video:

“OpenClaw Complete Setup Guide (Memory + Identity + Tools)”

👉 What it covers:

Workspace structure

Identity file creation

Memory system (the key piece)

Tool/skill usage

Telegram integration (you already did this)

2. Memory System Deep Dive

Search: “OpenClaw memory system explained”

👉 Focus:

How memory is stored

When it gets written

Why memory “doesn’t stick” (common issue)

3. Agent Identity + Personality Setup

Search: “OpenClaw agent identity file tutorial”

👉 Focus:

Defining:

Name

Role

Goals

Behavior constraints

📚 Written Guides (more precise than videos)
1. Official Quick Start (verify your setup)

Search: “OpenClaw quick start guide openclaw wiki”

👉 Critical step most people miss:

Enabling memory during onboarding/config

2. Beginner Docs Hub

Search: “OpenClaw beginner docs hub”

👉 Use this to:

Confirm folder structure

Understand config files

Fix broken setups

3. Full Getting Started Tutorial

Search: “OpenClaw complete getting started tutorial memory identity”

👉 Best for:

Debugging issues

Understanding how pieces connect

🧠 The missing piece (what you actually need to configure)

You said:

“I don’t know how to do the rest”

Here’s the exact minimum setup you need:

1. Identity file (REQUIRED)

Create something like:

JSON
{
  "name": "Atlas",
  "role": "AI assistant",
  "purpose": "Help the user solve problems efficiently",
  "personality": "Concise, practical, direct",
  "goals": [
    "Provide useful answers",
    "Take action when possible",
    "Remember important user context"
  ]
}
2. Memory (this is what you’re missing)

You need:

A. Memory enabled in config

Look for something like:

JSON
"memory": {
  "enabled": true,
  "type": "local"
}
B. Memory hook / skill enabled

If memory isn’t “writing,” it’s usually because:

The memory skill isn’t active

Or it’s not being triggered

C. Give it a reason to remember

OpenClaw won’t magically store everything.

You need prompts like:

“Remember that my name is Ben”

“Save this preference…”

3. Workspace structure (must be correct)

Typical layout:

/workspace
  /identity
  /memory
  /skills
  config.json

If this is wrong → things silently fail.

4. Purpose (this is what makes it feel “alive”)

Without this, it acts like a dumb chatbot.

Add in identity:

JSON
"purpose": "Act as a persistent AI agent that helps the user across sessions and builds long-term context"
⚠️ Common problems (you likely hit one of these)

Memory not enabled in config

Identity file not loaded

Wrong folder structure

Memory skill not triggered

Expecting automatic memory (it’s not)

✔️ If you want a clean working setup

I can generate you a complete ready-to-drop OpenClaw config:

identity.json

config.json

memory setup

example prompts

Just tell me:
👉 “
